articles.title: Finding a parabola through two points with given slopes
articles.titleZh: 求解过两点且给定斜率的抛物线
articles.summary: John D. Cook探讨了Artzt抛物线的数学原理——这类特殊抛物线需满足两个条件：通过指定两点，且在每点处的切线方向与给定直线平行。文章推导了这类问题的通用解法，涉及二次曲线的一般形式ax² + bxy + cy²。该研究有助于理解现代三角形几何中的特殊曲线构造方法。
articles.summaryEn: This mathematical exploration focuses on Artzt parabolas, which pass through two specified points with tangents parallel to given lines. The solution involves solving for coefficients in the general conic equation ax² + bxy + cy² to satisfy these geometric constraints. The derivation provides insight into advanced curve-fitting techniques used in modern triangle geometry.
articles.reason: 对于从事计算几何或数学建模的开发者来说，这是一篇难得的实用数学推导教程。

fullText.siteName: John D. Cook | Applied Mathematics Consulting

blocks.text: The Wikipedia article on modern triangle geometry has an image labeled “Artzt parabolas” with no explanation.
A quick search didn’t turn up anything about Artzt parabolas [1], but apparently the parabolas go through pairs of vertices with tangents parallel to the sides.
The general form of a conic section is
ax² + bxy + cy² + dx + ey + f = 0
and the constraint b² = 4ac means the conic will be a parabola.

We have 6 parameters, each determined only up to a scaling factor; you can multiply both sides by any non-zero constant and still have the same conic. So a general conic has 5 degrees of freedom, and the parabola condition b² = 4ac takes us down to 4. Specifying two points that the parabola passes through takes up 2 more degrees of freedom, and specifying the slopes takes up the last two. So it’s plausible that there is a unique solution to the problem.
There is indeed a solution, unique up to scaling the parameters. The following code finds parameters of a parabola that passes through (xi, yi) with slope mi for i = 1, 2.

def solve(x1, y1, m1, x2, y2, m2):
    
    Δx = x2 - x1
    Δy = y2 - y1
    λ = 4*(Δx*m1 - Δy)*(Δx*m2 - Δy)/(m1 - m2)**2
    k = x2*y1 - x1*y2

    a = Δy**2 + λ*m1*m2
    b = -2*Δx*Δy - λ*(m1 + m2)
    c = Δx**2 + λ
    d =  2*k*Δy + λ*(m1*y2 + m2*y1 - m1*m2*(x1 + x2))
    e = -2*k*Δx + λ*(m1*x1 + m2*x2 - y1 - y2)
    f = k**2 + λ*(m1*x1 - y1)*(m2*x2 - y2)

    return (a, b, c, d, e, f)
[1] The page said “Artz” when I first looked at it, but it has since been corrected to “Artzt”. Maybe I didn’t find anything because I was looking for the wrong spelling.

articles.title: Amazon to Acquire Globalstar, Announces Agreement With Apple to Continue Service for iPhone and Apple Watch
articles.titleZh: 亚马逊收购Globalstar并将为iPhone和Apple Watch提供卫星服务
articles.summary: 亚马逊宣布与Globalstar达成收购协议，计划将其低地球轨道卫星网络与直接到设备(D2D)服务整合。同时与苹果达成协议，由亚马逊Leo卫星网络为iPhone和Apple Watch提供包括紧急SOS在内的卫星通信服务。这项合作将显著扩展偏远地区的蜂窝覆盖范围。
articles.summaryEn: Amazon has agreed to acquire Globalstar to integrate direct-to-device (D2D) services into its low Earth orbit satellite network. In partnership with Apple, Amazon Leo will provide satellite connectivity for iPhone and Apple Watch, including Emergency SOS, extending cellular coverage to remote areas beyond terrestrial networks. This strategic move enhances global emergency communication capabilities.
articles.reason: 这是卫星互联网与消费电子巨头深度绑定的里程碑事件，预示着下一代应急通信基础设施的发展方向。

blocks.text: Globalstar satellites, radio frequency spectrum, and operational expertise will enable Amazon Leo to add Direct-to-Device (D2D) services to future generations of its low Earth orbit satellite network.
New Amazon Leo D2D system will help mobile network operators extend voice, text, and data services to customers beyond the reach of terrestrial cellular networks.

Amazon and Apple enter agreement for Amazon Leo to power satellite services for supported iPhone and Apple Watch models, allowing users to text emergency services, message friends and family, request roadside assistance, and more.

Today Amazon.com, Inc. and Globalstar, Inc. announced that they have entered into a definitive merger agreement under which Amazon will acquire Globalstar, enabling Amazon Leo to add direct-to-device (D2D) services to its low Earth orbit satellite network and extend cellular coverage to customers beyond the reach of terrestrial networks. In addition, Amazon and Apple announced an agreement for Amazon Leo to power satellite services for iPhone and Apple Watch1, including Emergency SOS via satellite. The new capabilities are part of Amazon’s long-term vision for space-based connectivity, and Amazon plans to work with mobile network operators (MNOs) and additional partners to deliver on that vision and extend reliable, high-speed connectivity to customers, no matter where they are in the world.

“There are billions of customers out there living, traveling, and operating in places beyond the reach of existing networks, and we started Amazon Leo to help bridge that divide,” said Panos Panay, Senior Vice President of Devices & Services, Amazon. “By combining Globalstar’s proven expertise and strong foundation with Amazon’s customer-obsession and innovation, customers can expect faster, more reliable service in more places—keeping them connected to the people and things that matter most. We’re excited to support Apple users through the Leo D2D system, and look forward to working with mobile network partners to help extend coverage to every corner of the planet.”
Amazon acquires Globalstar

Globalstar is a leading mobile satellite services (MSS) operator, a pioneer in non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellites and D2D technology, and a provider of critical and emergency communications to customers around the world. As part of the agreement, Amazon will acquire Globalstar’s existing satellite operations, infrastructure, and assets, including MSS spectrum licenses with global authorizations. Combining Globalstar’s spectrum and established MSS capabilities with the scale, performance, and reach of Amazon Leo will enable Amazon to deliver continuous connectivity for consumer, enterprise, and government customers around the world—whether they’re living or working in remote areas or simply moving in and out of traditional cellular networks. Globalstar’s existing satellite fleet and its new satellites with expanded capabilities will operate alongside the Amazon Leo broadband system and Amazon’s planned direct-to-device satellite system.

“We have long believed low Earth orbit satellite constellations offer the most effective path to truly connect users and devices anywhere and anytime,” said Paul Jacobs, CEO, Globalstar. “For more than 30 years, Globalstar has executed on this vision through sustained, long-term investment in technological innovation, operational excellence, and development of globally harmonized spectrum across both satellite and terrestrial applications. The combination with Amazon Leo will advance innovations in digital connectivity that will benefit our customers and advance us toward a more intelligent, continuously connected world.”
Amazon Leo Direct-to-Device (D2D)

Beginning in 2028, Amazon Leo will deploy its own next-generation D2D satellite system, allowing Amazon to deliver more advanced voice, data, and messaging services to mobile phones and other cellular devices. The Leo D2D system will offer substantially higher spectrum use and efficiency than legacy direct-to-cell systems, which translates into faster speeds and better performance for customers. It will also integrate seamlessly with Amazon’s first- and second-generation Leo systems, forming a powerful, unified network that combines fixed and mobile satellite services to support a wide range of customers and use cases. The complete Amazon Leo network will include thousands of advanced satellites in low Earth orbit and have enough capacity to support hundreds of millions of customer endpoints around the world.
Amazon Leo to power Apple satellite features

In addition to the agreement with Globalstar, Amazon and Apple signed an agreement to provide satellite connectivity for current and future iPhone and Apple Watch features. Globalstar currently partners with Apple to power satellite service on iPhone 14 or later, as well as Apple Watch Ultra 3, allowing users to text emergency services, message friends and family, request roadside assistance, and share their location. With the new Amazon-Apple agreement, Amazon will continue to support iPhone and Apple Watch models currently using Globalstar’s existing and planned upcoming low Earth orbit satellite constellations, being manufactured by MDA Space, and collaborate with Apple on future satellite services using Amazon Leo's expanded satellite network.

“Since launching more than three years ago, our groundbreaking safety service Emergency SOS via satellite has helped save many lives around the world—from a scout troop stranded on a winter hike in British Columbia, to a woman who was airlifted to safety in Colorado after her car rolled down a 250-foot cliff,” said Greg Joswiak, Senior Vice President of Worldwide Product Marketing, Apple. “Apple and Amazon have a long and proven track record of working together through Amazon’s core infrastructure services, and we look forward to building on that collaboration with Amazon Leo. This ensures our users will continue to have access to the vital satellite features they have come to rely on, including Emergency SOS, Messages, Find My, and Roadside Assistance via satellite, so they can stay safe and connected while off the grid.”
Connecting more customers globally
The agreements with Globalstar and Apple will promote innovation and competition across the space, satellite, and telecommunications sector, and support efforts to close the digital divide globally:

Accelerates innovation and expands connectivity options: By combining Amazon's low Earth orbit satellite network with Globalstar's infrastructure and spectrum assets, the acquisition enables faster deployment of D2D connectivity at scale—reaching areas where terrestrial deployment is delayed, cost-prohibitive, or vulnerable to disruption. It will also mean more reliable mobile data and communications services to consumers, businesses, and governments, while setting new benchmarks for innovation and giving customers greater choice, flexibility, and value in a dynamic and highly competitive satellite communications sector.

Strengthens resilience for private and public sector: D2D satellite connectivity provides critical fallback capabilities when terrestrial networks fail during hurricanes, wildfires, floods, and other disasters—and its utility extends well beyond emergency situations. Higher-capacity connectivity materially improves emergency response coordination while also enabling a broad range of everyday and mission-critical use cases, from emergency messaging, search-and-rescue, and maritime distress, to remote workforce connectivity, continuity of government operations, and rural broadband extension. In day-to-day operations, D2D service closes coverage gaps that terrestrial networks alone cannot address.

Drives economic growth and closes the digital divide: Amazon's multibillion-dollar investment in Amazon Leo is already creating broad economic opportunity across the U.S., Europe, and other regions around the world. These new agreements with Globalstar and Apple expand that roadmap, supporting high-value jobs in engineering, manufacturing, and operations while extending connectivity to enterprises, IoT applications, fleets, and supply chains operating beyond terrestrial reach—enabling productivity gains, new business models, and connecting underserved populations globally.

Key Transaction TermsUnder the terms of the merger agreement, prior to closing, Globalstar stockholders will elect to receive for each share of Globalstar common stock they own either (i) $90.00 in cash or (ii) 0.3210 shares of Amazon common stock with a value capped at $90.00 per share. This consideration is subject to a proration mechanism that caps aggregate cash elections to a maximum of 40% of total Globalstar shares, and automatically converts excess cash consideration into stock consideration on a pro rata basis. The total transaction consideration is also subject to a downward adjustment of a maximum $110 million in the event Globalstar does not achieve certain operational milestones.

Globalstar stockholders holding approximately 58% of the combined voting power of the outstanding shares of Globalstar common stock have approved the transaction by written consent. The transaction is expected to close in 2027, subject to the satisfaction of certain closing conditions, including receipt of regulatory approvals and the achievement by Globalstar of certain HIBLEO-4 replacement satellite milestones.

About AmazonAmazon is guided by four principles: customer obsession rather than competitor focus, passion for invention, commitment to operational excellence, and long-term thinking. Amazon strives to be Earth’s Most Customer-Centric Company, Earth’s Best Employer, and Earth’s Safest Place to Work. Customer reviews, 1-Click shopping, personalized recommendations, Prime, Fulfillment by Amazon, AWS, Kindle Direct Publishing, Kindle, Career Choice, Fire tablets, Fire TV, Amazon Echo, Alexa, Just Walk Out technology, Amazon Studios, and The Climate Pledge are some of the things pioneered by Amazon. For more information, visit amazon.com/about and follow @AmazonNews.

About Amazon LeoAmazon Leo is Amazon’s low Earth orbit satellite network. Its mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet to customers beyond the reach of existing networks, from individual households and small businesses to large enterprise and government customers—and anyone in between. Amazon Leo is powered by an initial constellation of more than 3,000 satellites, connected to a secure, global network of ground gateway antennas and dedicated fiber, and includes a lineup of compact, high-performance antennas—Leo Nano, Leo Pro, and Leo Ultra—that communicate with satellites passing overhead. The entire system is designed, built, and operated in-house at Amazon, and aims to connect tens of millions of customers around the world. Learn more about Amazon Leo.

About Globalstar, Inc.Globalstar is a global telecommunications provider connecting what matters most. Through our industry-leading low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellation and licensed Band 53/n53 spectrum, we deliver reliable satellite and terrestrial connectivity solutions that empower customers worldwide to connect, transmit, and communicate smarter.Our comprehensive connectivity ecosystem includes software-defined, purpose-built private wireless network platform, coupled with Globalstar Band 53™ in XCOM RAN™ and trusted GPS messengers Saved by SPOT™ for safety and personal communication for business and enterprise applications.

Serving business, enterprise, and consumer markets across the globe, Globalstar supports applications that track and protect assets, enable automation, enhance operational efficiency, and safeguard lives. With unmatched reach and a relentless focus on innovation, and mission-critical performance, we're redefining what's possible for global connectivity.For more information, visit www.globalstar.com.

translations: 全球星卫星、射频频谱和运营专长将使亚马逊“利奥”（Amazon Leo）在其低地球轨道卫星网络的未来几代产品中增加直接到设备（D2D）服务。
新的亚马逊“利奥”D2D系统将帮助移动网络运营商向那些无法接入地面蜂窝网络的客户提供语音、文本和数据服务。
亚马逊与苹果达成协议，由亚马逊“利奥”为支持的iPhone和Apple Watch机型提供卫星服务，使用户能够通过卫星发送紧急求救信息、与亲友联系、请求 roadside assistance（道路救援）等。

今天，亚马逊公司（Amazon.com, Inc.）与全球星公司（Globalstar, Inc.）宣布已达成最终合并协议，根据该协议，亚马逊将收购全球星，使亚马逊“利奥”能够在低地球轨道卫星网络上增加直接到设备（D2D）服务，并将蜂窝网络覆盖范围扩展至地面网络无法到达的客户区域。此外，亚马逊与苹果还宣布了一项协议，由亚马逊“利奥”为iPhone和Apple Watch提供卫星服务，包括通过卫星实现的Emergency SOS（紧急求救）。这些新功能是亚马逊长期太空连接愿景的一部分，亚马逊计划与移动网络运营商（MNOs）及其他合作伙伴合作，实现这一愿景，为全球各地的客户——无论身处何地——提供可靠的高速连接。

“世界上有数十亿客户生活在、旅行于或工作在现有网络无法覆盖的地区，我们启动亚马逊‘利奥’项目正是为了帮助弥合这一差距。”亚马逊设备与服务高级副总裁潘纳·帕纳伊（Panos Panay）表示。“通过将全球星经过验证的专业知识和坚实基础与亚马逊以客户为中心的专注和创新相结合，客户将能在更多地方享受到更快、更可靠的连接服务——让他们始终与最重要的人和事物保持联系。我们很高兴通过‘利奥’D2D系统支持苹果用户，并期待与移动网络合作伙伴携手，帮助将网络覆盖拓展至地球的每一个角落。”
亚马逊收购全球星

全球星是一家领先的移动卫星服务（MSS）运营商，在非地球静止轨道（NGSO）卫星和直接到设备（D2D）技术方面处于先驱地位，并为全球客户提供关键的应急通信服务。根据该协议，亚马逊将获得全球星的现有卫星运营、基础设施和资产，包括具有全球授权许可的MSS频谱许可证。将全球星的频谱和成熟的MSS能力与亚马逊“利奥”的规模、性能和覆盖范围相结合，将使亚马逊能够为消费者、企业和政府机构客户提供持续的连接服务——无论他们是在偏远地区生活或工作，还是频繁进出传统蜂窝网络覆盖区域。全球星的现有卫星舰队及其具备增强功能的新型卫星将与亚马逊“利奥”宽带系统和亚马逊规划的直接到设备卫星系统协同运行。
“长期以来，我们始终坚信低地球轨道卫星星座是实现用户和设备随时随地真正连接的最有效路径，”Globalstar首席执行官保罗·雅各布表示。“三十多年来，Globalstar通过持续、长期的技术创新投资、卓越运营以及在全球范围内协调卫星和地面应用的频谱资源，持续推进这一愿景。与亚马逊Leo的合作将推动数字连接技术的革新，让我们的客户受益，并助力我们迈向一个更智能、持续互联的世界。”
亚马逊Leo直连设备（D2D）

自2028年起，亚马逊Leo将部署其下一代D2D卫星系统，使亚马逊能够为手机和其他蜂窝设备提供更先进的语音、数据和消息服务。Leo D2D系统的频谱利用率和效率将显著高于传统直连蜂窝系统，这意味着客户将获得更快的速度和更佳的性能表现。该系统还将与亚马逊第一代和第二代Leo系统无缝集成，形成一个强大的统一网络，融合固定和移动卫星服务，以支持广泛的客户群体和应用场景。完整的亚马逊Leo网络将包括数千颗先进低轨卫星，具备足够的容量为全球数亿个客户终端提供支持。
亚马逊Leo赋能苹果卫星功能

除了与Globalstar的合作协议外，亚马逊与苹果还签署了一项协议，为当前及未来的iPhone和Apple Watch提供卫星连接服务。目前，Globalstar已与苹果合作，为iPhone 14及后续机型以及Apple Watch Ultra 3提供卫星服务，使用户能够向紧急服务机构发送短信、与亲友联系、请求 roadside assistance（道路救援），并分享自己的位置信息。根据新的亚马逊-苹果协议，亚马逊将继续支持当前使用Globalstar现有及计划中的由MDA Space制造的低轨卫星星座的iPhone和Apple Watch机型，并与苹果共同开发基于亚马逊Leo扩展卫星网络的未来卫星服务。

“自三年前推出革命性的卫星紧急求救服务Emergency SOS via satellite以来，这项开创性服务已帮助全球无数人脱离险境——从被困在英属哥伦比亚冬季徒步路线上的童子军，到车辆滚落250英尺悬崖后被直升机救起的女子，”苹果公司全球产品营销高级副总裁格雷格·约斯维克表示。“苹果与亚马逊有着长期且经得起验证的合作历史，尤其是在亚马逊核心基础设施服务领域。我们期待通过与亚马逊Leo的合作进一步深化这种协作关系。这将确保我们的用户能够继续使用他们赖以依赖的关键卫星功能，包括通过卫星实现的Emergency SOS、Messages、Find My和Roadside Assistance，让他们在远离网络覆盖区域时也能保持安全并维持连接。”
连接更多全球客户
与Globalstar和苹果达成的协议将促进航天、卫星和电信行业的创新与竞争，并支持全球缩小数字鸿沟的努力：
加速创新并扩展连接选项：通过将亚马逊的低地球轨道卫星网络与 Globalstar 的基础设施和频谱资产相结合，此次收购能够更快地大规模部署 D2D 连接——覆盖地面网络部署延迟、成本过高或易受干扰的地区。此举还将为消费者、企业和政府提供更可靠的移动数据和通信服务，同时在卫星通信这一动态且高度竞争的行业中树立创新新标杆，为客户带来更多选择、灵活性和价值。
增强公私领域的抗灾韧性：在飓风、野火、洪水等灾害导致地面网络中断时，D2D 卫星连接可提供关键的备用能力；其用途远不止于应急场景。更高容量的连接显著提升了应急响应协调效率，并支持广泛的日常和关键任务应用，包括紧急消息传递、搜救、海上遇险通信，以及远程员工连接、政府运作连续性保障和农村宽带延伸。在日常运营中，D2D 服务填补了仅靠地面网络无法覆盖的空白区域。
推动经济增长并弥合数字鸿沟：亚马逊对“亚马逊利奥”（Amazon Leo）的数十亿美元投资已在全美、欧洲及其他地区创造了广泛的经济机遇。与 Globalstar 和苹果的新协议进一步拓展了这一发展蓝图，通过在工程、制造和运营领域创造高价值岗位，为超越地面覆盖范围的企业、物联网应用、车队和供应链提供连接支持——从而提升生产力、催生新的商业模式，并连接全球尚未充分服务的群体。

交易核心条款：根据合并协议条款，交割前，Globalstar 股东可选择为其持有的每一股 Globalstar 普通股获得以下对价之一：（i）90.00 美元现金，或（ii）价值上限为每股 90.00 美元的 0.3210 股 Amazon 普通股。该对价受制于一项按比例分配机制，即现金选择总额不得超过 Globalstar 总股份的 40%，超出部分将自动转换为股票对价。此外，若 Globalstar 未能达成特定运营里程碑，交易总对价最多可向下调整 1.1 亿美元。
持有 Globalstar 已发行普通股约 58% 表决权的股东已通过书面同意批准该交易。该交易预计将于 2027 年完成，前提是满足若干交割条件，包括获得监管审批以及 Globalstar 完成 HIBLEO-4 替换卫星的关键里程碑。

关于亚马逊 亚马逊以四大原则为指导：以客户为中心而非竞争对手导向、对发明的激情、对卓越的运营承诺以及长期思维。亚马逊致力于成为地球上以客户为中心度最高的公司、地球上最好的雇主以及地球上最安全的工作场所。亚马逊开创了许多创新产品，包括客户评论、一键购物、个性化推荐、Prime会员服务、亚马逊物流（FBA）、AWS云服务、Kindle直接出版、Kindle电子书阅读器、Career Choice职业发展计划、Fire平板电脑、Fire TV、Amazon Echo智能音箱、Alexa语音助手、Just Walk Out无感支付技术、Amazon Studios影视制作以及《气候承诺》倡议等。更多信息请访问 amazon.com/about 并关注 @AmazonNews。

关于亚马逊利奥 亚马逊利奥是亚马逊的低地球轨道卫星网络。其使命是为现有网络无法覆盖的客户群体提供快速可靠的互联网服务，服务对象涵盖从个人家庭和小微企业到大型企业和政府机构的各类用户。亚马逊利奥由初始部署的3000多颗卫星星座构成，通过全球安全地面网关天线网络和专用光纤连接，并配备了一系列紧凑型高性能天线——利奥纳米（Leo Nano）、利奥专业（Leo Pro）和利奥超高性能（Leo Ultra），可与头顶经过的卫星进行通信。整个系统均由亚马逊自主研发、制造和运营，旨在为全球数千万客户提供连接服务。了解更多关于亚马逊利奥的信息。

关于全球星公司 全球星是一家全球电信服务提供商，致力于连接最重要的事物。凭借我们行业领先的低地球轨道（LEO）卫星星座和授权的Band 53/n53频谱资源，我们为客户提供可靠的卫星与地面混合连接解决方案，助力全球客户实现更智能的连接、传输与通信。我们的综合连接生态系统包括软件定义、专为特定用途设计的私有无线网络平台，结合Globalstar Band 53™技术与XCOM RAN™架构，以及用于保障安全和商务通信的SPOT™可信GPS信使服务。
全球星服务于遍布世界各地的商业、企业和消费市场，支持资产追踪保护、自动化控制、运营效率提升以及生命安全守护等应用。凭借无与伦比的覆盖范围和持续不断的创新精神，以及对关键任务性能的执着追求，我们正在重新定义全球连接的无限可能。更多信息请访问 www.globalstar.com。
